title: Suspicious inbox campaign response
business_context: A fictional workforce reports a coordinated suspicious-message campaign while identity telemetry shows unusual session behavior.
initial_stakes: Several sanitized training accounts show risky sign-in patterns and business operations need a controlled response.

turns.situation: Multiple users report similar suspicious messages and identity alerts are increasing.

options.label: Correlate identity and message telemetry
options.description: Confirm campaign scope before broad containment.
options.expected_outcome: The response team establishes a defensible incident scope.

options.label: Wait for more user reports
options.description: Delay triage until the campaign produces more evidence.
options.expected_outcome: Potential exposure expands while containment is delayed.

turns.situation: Telemetry identifies a bounded set of suspicious sessions and message indicators.

options.label: Contain suspicious sessions and preserve evidence
options.description: Revoke affected sessions while retaining investigation artifacts.
options.expected_outcome: Active exposure is reduced without losing investigation context.

options.label: Reset every workforce account
options.description: Apply broad disruption before validating affected scope.
options.expected_outcome: Operational disruption grows beyond the validated scope.

turns.situation: Business leaders need a clear update while investigation confidence is still developing.

options.label: Open incident command with scoped facts
options.description: Communicate known impact, uncertainty, and next checkpoint.
options.expected_outcome: Stakeholders align without overstating investigation certainty.

options.label: Announce a confirmed breach
options.description: State a conclusion before evidence supports it.
options.expected_outcome: The response creates avoidable trust and governance risk.

turns.situation: Containment is holding and analysts must validate whether persistence remains.

options.label: Correlate sign-ins, session revocation, and endpoint signals
options.description: Verify that suspicious activity has stopped across controls.
options.expected_outcome: The team gains evidence that containment is effective.

options.label: Close the incident after session revocation
options.description: End response before checking for continued activity.

turns.situation: No new suspicious sessions appear and the response must transition to recovery.

options.label: Restore access in stages and publish recovery guidance
options.description: Re-enable affected workflows with monitoring and clear workforce instructions.
options.expected_outcome: Operations resume with monitored security controls.

options.label: Remove all temporary controls immediately
options.description: Return to baseline without a monitored recovery window.
options.expected_outcome: Recovery proceeds without evidence that controls remain effective.
